checks.a_analysis: The output follows the structure and content of the source document accurately without adding any fabricated text, introductions, or conclusions. All headings and list items in the output are present in the source.

There is no fabricated contact information. While several URLs and links from the source (e.g., SMAC website, NC OneMap website, and 'Join meeting' link) are missing from the output, no new or incorrect information was added.
All primary sections of the agenda (Welcome, Framework+ Datasets, Working Groups, Regular Status Updates, Other Business, Action Item Review, Adjourn) are represented in the output.
The document contains lists but no formal tables. The list of meeting dates is correctly represented.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The output contains significant omissions. Specifically, all nested list items (sub-bullets) across multiple sections are completely missing. This includes updates on Contours/DEM, County/Municipal boundaries, the NC Board on Geographic Names discussion items (Simone/Stiles Mountain), and the Metadata Committee update. Additionally, the website URLs in the header and footer are missing. The total content loss exceeds 25% of the agenda detail.
checks.b_evidence_source: Updates on Contours and DEM QC and “sealing”; County Boundaries - Gary Thompson; Municipal Boundaries – Sarah Wray; SMAC Discussion and Approval; Simone Mountain; Stiles Mountain; Update on refresh of documents/tools on NC OneMap (www.nconemap.gov/pages/metadata); Adjustment to reflect the correct statutes and bring bylaws in line with those of other subcommittees; SMAC Web Site – http://it.nc.gov/gicc | NC OneMap Web Site - www.nconemap.gov
checks.b_evidence_output: The nested_list property is null for all items, and the text for these sub-bullets is entirely absent.
checks.b_evidence_location: Sections: Framework+ Datasets, Working Groups, Other Business, and Document Header/Footer.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: There are minor text omissions that alter the completeness of specific items. The word 'minutes' is missing from the first action item, and the text 'Join meeting' is missing from the Web Conference line.
checks.b_evidence_source: Approval of October 14, 2020 minutes; Web Conference: Join meeting
checks.b_evidence_location: Welcome/Introductions (element reading_order 6) and Web Conference info (reading_order 3).

checks.a_analysis: I have reviewed the output against the source document. Each section and list item in the JSON corresponds to a single instance in the source PDF. There are no duplicated text blocks or repeated sections.

The document title is correctly assigned H1. The major section headings (Welcome, Framework, Working Groups, etc.) are correctly assigned H2. The sidebar '2021 Meetings' is assigned H3, which is a logical subsection level given its position. The hierarchy follows a logical structure without skipping levels.

The source document does not contain formal tables. The '2021 Meetings' section, which is a tabular list of dates, has been converted into a heading and a paragraph, which effectively preserves its informational structure for this document type.

The reading order in the JSON follows the logical top-to-bottom, left-to-right flow of the source document, including correctly placing the sidebar content ('2021 Meetings') after the section it is visually adjacent to.

The source document contains several instances of nested lists (sub-bullets). The JSON output fails to capture any of these nested items. For example, under 'Framework+ Datasets', sub-items for 'Elevation' and 'Administrative/Governmental Units' are missing. Under 'Working Groups', sub-items for 'NC Board on Geographic Names' and 'Metadata Committee' are missing. Under 'Other Business', the sub-bullet describing bylaws adjustments is missing. This is a major failure in structural fidelity.
checks.b_evidence_source:
Elevation – John Lay
    o Updates on Contours and DEM QC and “sealing”
Administrative/Governmental Units
    o County Boundaries - Gary Thompson
    o Municipal Boundaries – Sarah Wray

checks.a_analysis: Multiple links and URLs present in the source are missing in the output. The 'minutes' link in the first section is truncated. The 'Join meeting' link text and functionality in the header are missing. Furthermore, the entire banner line containing the SMAC and NC OneMap website URLs is omitted from the conversion.
checks.b_evidence_source:
Approval of October 14, 2020 minutes
Web Conference: Join meeting
SMAC Web Site – http://it.nc.gov/gicc | NC OneMap Web Site - www.nconemap.gov

checks.a_analysis: The visual organization of the document is significantly compromised by the omission of all nested list items (sub-bullets). In the source PDF, the agenda relies on a hierarchical structure to categorize specific topics under main bullet points. The HTML output fails to render these nested levels, resulting in a flat list where the sub-points (e.g., discussion items under 'NC Board on Geographic Names' or updates under 'Elevation') are entirely absent. This constitutes a major failure in preserving the visual organization of the information.
checks.b_evidence_source: Elevation – John Lay followed by sub-bullet 'Updates on Contours and DEM QC and “sealing”'; and 'NC Board on Geographic Names - Cam McNutt' followed by nested items 'SMAC Discussion and Approval', 'Simone Mountain', and 'Stiles Mountain'.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML screenshot shows 'Elevation - John Lay' and 'NC Board on Geographic Names - Cam McNutt' as single-level bullet points with no nested items following them.
checks.b_evidence_location: Framework+ Datasets and Working Groups sections, Page 1 of PDF.

checks.a_analysis: The source PDF does not contain any tables. The '2021 Meetings' section is a simple list of dates, which is rendered correctly as a list in the HTML.
The source PDF consists entirely of text and basic formatting. There are no meaningful images, logos, or charts that require rendering in HTML.
Aside from the Table of Contents (which is a standard accessibility feature for the HTML output), no body content is duplicated in the rendering.
